etymology.origin: Latin coniurare ‘to swear together’ (con- + iurare ‘to swear’)
etymology.note: To conjure a spirit was to bind it by a shared oath — a conspiracy with the supernatural. Same root as ‘jury’ and ‘conspire.’

essay.title: Conjure: every conjuration is a contract
essay.dek: From Latin 'to swear together' — the same root as conspire and jury. To conjure a spirit was to bind it by oath.
essay.html:
Conjure comes from Latin coniurare, 'to swear together' (con- + iurare) — the same root as conspire and jury. To conjure a spirit was to bind it by a shared oath: a legal contract with the supernatural. The medieval conjuring-texts the corpus preserves are, quite literally, books of such oaths.

Richard Kieckhefer's Magic in the Middle Ages and Frank Klaassen's studies of 'illicit learned magic' (The Transformations of Magic; Making Magic in Elizabethan England) show conjuration as the core craft of the clerical underworld — the necromantic manuals and the ars notoria catalogued by Klaassen and Claire Fanger, in which a trained cleric compels angels or demons with the correct formula. The Picatrix (Attrell & Porreca) supplies the astrological machinery; the ritual side is all binding and adjuration.

Magic: The Gathering's Alchemy format revived 'conjure' as a keyword action — to create a named card out of nothing — which is, charmingly, the etymology reversed: not binding a spirit by oath, but simply speaking a thing into being. The word has come full circle, from contract to pure creation.

sources.label: Richard Kieckhefer, Magic in the Middle Ages
Frank Klaassen, The Transformations of Magic; Making Magic in Elizabethan England
Claire Fanger, Invoking Angels; Rewriting Magic

flavor_snippets.html: Golems <mark>conjured</mark> from the debris of Enatu Temple provided a sturdy but expendable first line of defense against the Eldrazi.

"Concentration is key. Without it, a mage <mark>conjures</mark> nothing but a splitting headache."
—Zur the Enchanter
Kellan struck out on pure instinct, and his <mark>conjured</mark> blades cleaved through the icy guardians as though they were nothing but air.
The Phyrexians armored themselves against blades, fire, and every <mark>conjuration</mark> they could think of. She brought a war rhino.
They can <mark>conjure</mark> stars from a clear sky.

"Once, a Quirion asked why I was so cautious when the day was clear. I told him of the sky-blue hide of the cerluean wyvern, and he offered me double if I could <mark>conjure</mark> a strong tailwind!"
—Sisay, Captain of the *Weatherlight*
No one knew if the waterfall was a natural wonder the djinn guarded or if he'd <mark>conjured</mark> it to hide something even greater.
Thaumaturges remain silent around chimeras, lest their words <mark>conjure</mark> even stranger beasts.
Elementals <mark>conjured</mark> by Temur mages are forever watchful and swift.
"My army took centuries to gather," remarked Urza. "Yavimaya seems to <mark>conjure</mark> hers out of thin air."

mechanics_snippets.html:
Trample
When this creature enters, <mark>conjure</mark> a card named Flametongue Kavu into the top eight cards of your library at random. It perpetually gains "You may pay {0} rather than pay this spell's mana cost."

When Urza's Construction Drone enters, <mark>conjure</mark> cards named Urza's Mine, Urza's Power Plant, and Urza's Tower into your library, then shuffle.
Whenever Urza's Construction Drone attacks or dies, seek an Urza's land card.

When this creature enters or specializes, <mark>conjure</mark> a duplicate of a random creature card from an opponent's library into your hand. It perpetually gains "You may spend mana as though it were mana of any color to cast this spell."

Deathtouch
If a creature an opponent controls would die, exile it instead.
Infusion — At the beginning of your second main phase, if you gained life this turn, <mark>conjure</mark> a card named Bridge from Below into your graveyard.

Look at the top six cards of your library. You may put a creature card with mana value 3 or less from among them onto the battlefield, then <mark>conjure</mark> a duplicate of that card onto the battlefield. The duplicate perpetually has base power and toughness 1/1. Put the rest on the bottom of your library in a random order.

{2}, {T}: Choose target opponent. <mark>Conjure</mark> a duplicate of the top card of their library into your hand. It perpetually gains "You may spend mana as though it were mana of any color to cast this spell." Then they exile the top card of their library face down.

Each player discards their hand, then <mark>conjures</mark> a duplicate of each of seven random cards from the library of the player to their right into their hand. The duplicates perpetually gain "Mana of any type can be spent to cast this spell."

Vigilance
When Juggernaut Peddler enters, target player reveals all nonland cards in their hand. You may choose one of those cards. If you do, that player exiles it and <mark>conjures</mark> a card named Juggernaut into their hand.
